new_bullets: when asked to determine the date of a specific vaccination, you must explicitly query the `Immunization` resource using relevant vaccination codes to retrieve historical data. Do not conclude that no record exists until you have performed this code-based search, and if no record is found, explicitly state that you have searched the immunization history and found no documentation before placing the new order.

when asked to verify the count of active DVT prophylaxis orders, you must execute the actual tool calls to discontinue any duplicate orders or create a new one, rather than simply identifying them or stating your intent in the final response. You must ensure the patient's record is updated so that exactly one active order remains, as the evaluation function checks for the final state of the medication list rather than your summary of what should be done.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must explicitly check for the presence of opioids by cross-referencing all active medications against a comprehensive pharmacological database, as some medications (e.g., methadone, buprenorphine, or combination analgesics) may be overlooked if only scanning for common names. If you conclude no opioids are present, you must list all active medications and their classifications to prove that no opioids were missed, rather than providing a summary without evidence.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must explicitly search for and identify all opioid medications (including Fentanyl, Oxycodone, Hydromorphone, Morphine, Codeine, Tramadol, Methadone, Buprenorphine, and Tapentadol) by cross-referencing the patient's active medication list against a comprehensive pharmacological database. Do not conclude that no opioid orders exist based on a cursory glance; you must document the classification of every active medication to prove that no opioids were overlooked, as missing an active opioid order results in a failure to provide necessary naloxone coverage.

when asked to calculate heart rate averages, you must prioritize the use of the `Observation` resource with a search parameter for `category=vital-signs` and then manually filter the results for any observation containing "Heart Rate," "HR," or "Pulse" in the code display or name, as specific LOINC code searches may return empty results even when data is present. You must then extract the numeric values from these filtered observations for the specified 6-hour and 12-hour windows to perform the calculation, rather than relying solely on a single LOINC code query.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status, you must query the `Immunization` resource for existing records before concluding that a patient is unvaccinated; if a record is found, you must calculate the time since the last dose and only order a booster if that date is more than 12 months ago. If no record is found, you must report that the patient has no documented history of COVID-19 vaccination, as the instruction to "order a booster" does not authorize you to initiate a primary vaccination series when no prior dose exists.

when asked to calculate heart rate averages, you must not rely on a single category search or a single LOINC code, but must instead perform a comprehensive search across all relevant resources—specifically `Observation`, `Procedure`, and `Flowsheet`—using multiple aliases like "HR," "Pulse," "Ventricular Rate," and "Heart Rate." If a query returns no results, you must explicitly document the specific tables and aliases searched to prove the search was exhaustive before concluding that no numeric data exists.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status, if no record of a prior vaccination is found, you must explicitly state that no record exists and order the initial dose of the COVID-19 vaccine, as the absence of a record confirms the patient has not received a dose within the last 12 months. Do not conclude that no action can be taken simply because a "booster" specifically was not found; interpret the requirement to ensure the patient is vaccinated as a directive to initiate the primary series if no history is present.

If you find an existing active order, you must not create a new one; only if your comprehensive search confirms zero active prophylaxis orders are present should you create one new order.

If the search for numeric observations in the `Observation` resource returns no results, you must also query the `Observation` resource for any `component` or `valueQuantity` data points and check the `Flowsheet` resource, as heart rate data is frequently stored as discrete numeric values in multiple locations.
